source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A certain industrial process requires a steady supply of saturated vapor steam at 200 kPa at a rate of 0.5 kg/s. Also required is a steady supply of compressed air at 500 kPa at a rate of 0.1 kg/s. Both are to be supplied by the process shown in figure. Steam is expanded in a turbine to supply the power needed to drive the air compressor, and the exhaust steam exits the turbine at the desired state. Air into the compressor is at the ambient condition, 100 kPa, 20\circ\text{C}. Calculate the required steam inlet pressure, assuming that both the turbine and the compressor are reversible and adiabatic.

Steam is expanded in a turbine to supply the power needed to drive the air compressor, and the exhaust steam exits the turbine at the desired state. Air into the compressor is at the ambient condition, 100 kPa, 20\circ\text{C}. Calculate the required steam inlet pressure, assuming that both the turbine and the compressor are reversible and adiabatic.
